steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says the microchip “fundamentally ______ the industry by enabling it to take advantage of mass production methods for the first time.” The key phrase is enabling it to take advantage … for the first time — this describes a positive, enabling change that unlocked new capabilities. The blank must express a fundamental shift, not obstruction or avoidance.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'obstructed': Means to block or hinder. This contradicts the idea of enabling access to mass production — opposite of what the sentence describes. ✗
  • Choice B — 'transformed': Means to fundamentally change the nature or character of something. Pairing it with “fundamentally” and a clause about gaining new capabilities for the first time captures a deep, enabling change. ✓
  • Choice C — 'bypassed': Means to go around or evade. This suggests the industry avoided mass production rather than gaining access to it — the wrong direction. ✗
  • Choice D — 'overwhelmed': Means to overpower or flood. This implies a negative, destructive effect rather than the enabling change the sentence describes. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
